Introduction
Personal Presentation

Hi, my name is Abel Castillo. I am from Panama City, Panama, where I was born and raised.

I am currently working as an Administrative Assistant at Keene's Ice Creams (Marbella branch). At the same time, I am studying a Técnico Superior in Big Data and Data Science at ITSE, where I am in my 4th semester.

In my free time, I enjoy singing and spending quality time with my family and friends. I also love taking care of my dog, Luffy.

I am interested in data science, technology, and digital entrepreneurship. One of my main goals is to build my own consulting firm in the future, combining my professional experience in operations with my knowledge in Big Data.

This portfolio reflects my learning journey throughout this English course. It includes the five units we studied, the workshops I completed, and the quizzes I took. I hope it demonstrates my progress and commitment to improving my English skills.

Abel Omar Castillo · 8-890-156 · BD-4-1
Week 2 – 4
Unit 1  ·  Living in a Neighborhood
Book: FUTURE · Unit 1
Objectives
What I Learned
  • Identify different countries and languages
  • Make small talk with new people
  • Talk about places in the neighborhood
  • Follow written instructions
  • Talk about holidays and celebrations
  • Academic skill: Understand the main idea
  • Writing skill: Write a topic sentence
  • Workplace soft skill: Be inclusive
  • Vocabulary for countries, languages, and neighborhood places
  • How to make small talk and introduce myself naturally
  • Simple Present tense: affirmative, negative, questions
  • Adverbs of frequency and their correct position in a sentence
  • How to write a paragraph with a clear topic sentence
  • Be inclusive as a workplace value

Week 5 – 7
Unit 2  ·  Setting Goals
Book: FUTURE · Unit 2
Objectives
What I Learned
  • Identify and talk about goals for the future
  • Identify steps to a better job
  • Set realistic goals with time frames
  • Talk about obstacles and supports
  • Identify community services
  • Academic skill: Predict
  • Writing skill: Write supporting sentences
  • Workplace soft skill: Take responsibility for professional growth
  • Goal vocabulary: apply for financial aid, get a certificate, get a promotion, take citizenship classes
  • Future with will (certainty) and might (possibility)
  • Contractions: will not = won't
  • Setting goals with realistic time frames
  • Difference between obstacles and supports
  • Writing skill: supporting sentences using first, then, next, finally
📎 Evidence – Workshop 2: Daily Routines & Adverbs of Frequency
Workshop 2 – Video

Daily Routines – Oral Presentation

I recorded a video speech describing my daily routines using Simple Present Tense and adverbs of frequency, presenting 10+ routines with pictures — without reading from any notes.

  • ✅ 10+ daily routines with pictures
  • ✅ Adverbs of frequency used (always, usually, sometimes, never…)
  • ✅ Simple Present Tense applied correctly
  • ✅ Spoken from memory — no reading
  • ✅ Dressed appropriately

Quiz 1 – Writing

My Goal – Using will, won't, might, might not

📊 Score: 16 / 20
"My goal is to have my own consulting firm. First, I have to improve my knowledge in Big Data and Data Science and then graduate from my career. I want to work in something related to data science after I graduate to obtain more experience in my area. In the same time, I have to save money to start my new business. In the beginning, maybe I will have to work alone, but later I want to have other colleagues."

Book: FUTURE · Unit 3
Objectives
What I Learned
  • Identify and talk about school activities
  • Help children with school work
  • Ask for time off from work
  • Communicate with a child's teacher

  • Writing skill: Support an opinion with reasons
  • Workplace soft skill: Separate work life and family life

  • How to write an opinion paragraph with supporting reasons

Week 13 – 15
Unit 5  ·  Traveling
Book: FUTURE · Unit 5
📎 Evidence – Final Assignment: Airline Customer Experience Analysis
Case Study – Final Assignment

Copa Airlines vs. American Airlines — Flying to Panama

This case study compares two major airlines that operate flights to Panama City: Copa Airlines and American Airlines. The goal is to analyze their customer experience across three key areas and propose technology-based improvements.

🔍 Research Areas

  • Customer Reviews: Analysis of passenger satisfaction, complaints, and ratings from platforms such as TripAdvisor, Skytrax, and Google Reviews
  • Pricing: Comparison of fare structures, baggage fees, and value for money on routes to Panama (PTY)
  • Technology: Evaluation of mobile apps, online check-in systems, seat selection tools, and digital boarding processes

⚖️ Strengths & Weaknesses Identified

Copa Airlines

✅ Strengths: Panama hub advantage, strong regional network, reliable on-time performance, modern fleet

⚠️ Weaknesses: Limited entertainment options, app UI needs improvement, fewer international partnerships

American Airlines

✅ Strengths: Extensive global network, advanced mobile app, wide seat selection, AAdvantage loyalty program

⚠️ Weaknesses: Higher pricing on Panama routes, inconsistent customer service reviews, baggage fee complaints

💡 Technology Improvement Suggestions

  • AI-powered chatbots for 24/7 customer support and real-time flight delay notifications
  • Automated check-in kiosks with facial recognition to reduce wait times at PTY airport
  • Improved mobile UI with personalized recommendations for seats, meals, and upgrades
  • Predictive analytics to anticipate delays and proactively rebook affected passengers
  • Digital luggage tracking integrated into the airline app for real-time bag location updates

📋 Learning Objectives

  • Develop critical thinking by identifying and analyzing real-world problems in the airline industry
  • Enhance research skills through comparison of airlines and their customer experiences
  • Foster creativity and innovation by proposing digital solutions to improve travel experiences
  • Strengthen presentation and communication skills through visual reports
  • Cultivate understanding of technology's role in customer service and operational efficiency
Abel Omar Castillo · 8-890-156 · BD-4-1
Conclusion
Personal Reflection

Throughout this English course, I have significantly improved my ability to communicate in English in both written and spoken form. Each unit presented new vocabulary, grammar structures, and real-life skills that I was able to connect with my personal and professional experiences.

In Unit 1, I strengthened my ability to talk about my neighborhood using Simple Present and adverbs of frequency. In Unit 2, I learned how to express future plans with will and might — a skill I directly applied when writing about my own professional goals. Unit 3 helped me master the Simple Past tense and gave me tools to write opinion paragraphs. Unit 4 was especially relevant to my professional life, focusing on job qualities, work history, and the Curriculum Vitae. Finally, Unit 5 expanded my vocabulary around travel and taught me how to navigate real-world airport situations in English.

The workshops pushed me beyond the classroom — delivering speeches, recording a video presentation, and publishing a blog were all challenging but rewarding experiences that built my confidence using English authentically.

I am grateful for the guidance of Professor Elisabet Jordan throughout this course. This portfolio reflects my dedication, growth, and commitment to continuous improvement in English.
